title: Salty Flaky Kenyan Potato Flatbread
description: A quick, savory flatbread with a crisp, flaky crust and a soft potato starch base-perfect for a weeknight Kenyan meal.

ingredients.name: potato starch
ingredients.notes: Use brown potato starch for authentic texture

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together potato starch, salt, pepper, and cumin powder until fully blended.
Gradually pour warm water into the dry mixture, whisking continuously until a smooth, lump-free dough forms.
Stir in lemon juice and vegetable oil until the dough becomes pliable and slightly sticky.
Cover the dough with a clean cloth and allow it to rest for 15 minutes to hydrate the starch.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and add a small splash of oil to coat the surface.
Divide the rested dough into four equal portions; roll each portion into a 1/4‑inch thick disc using a rolling pin or flat spatula.
Place each disc in the hot skillet and cook for 3–4 minutes on each side, or until a golden, flaky crust develops, pressing lightly to ensure even cooking.
Transfer the cooked flatbreads to a serving plate, sprinkle with chopped coriander if desired, and serve immediately while hot.
